decisions.title: Ship Explore V2 (E2) to GA with current feature flag coverage
decisions.context: Feature flag audit (ENG-9779) underway, candidate runs integration progressing (ENG-9533, ENG-9532, ENG-9537), annotation improvements landing (ENG-9981, ENG-9861). Need decision on GA timeline given remaining UX work (DES-163) and integration testing gaps (ENG-9585).

arguments.for: Core features stable, customer demand high, delayed GA risks competitive position. Can iterate UX post-launch. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar.
arguments.against: Checkly e2e testing not evaluated (ENG-9585), UX gaps in Run creation (DES-163), annotation bugs still surfacing (ENG-9861). Premature GA could damage trust.

decisions.title: Prioritize CSRS rollout over new Canvas features
decisions.context: CSRS (Columnar Storage Read Service) work accelerating: DataFusion reads (ENG-9493, ENG-9460), legacy format support (ENG-9988, ENG-9986, ENG-9987), shadow traffic ready (ENG-9494). This is foundational moat-building infrastructure vs. customer-visible Canvas work.

arguments.for: CSRS enables 10x query performance (moat), unblocks future scale, high eng momentum now. Canvas can wait 1 sprint without customer impact.
arguments.against: Canvas revenue pipeline depends on new visualizations, customer commitments at risk. CSRS benefits invisible until fully rolled out (months away).

decisions.title: Mandate 15-min session timeout for enterprise tier only
decisions.context: Session timeout work scoped (ENG-9304) but no product decision on rollout. Impacts all users vs. compliance-driven enterprise customers. Affects daily workflow friction.
decisions.blockedBy: Need security audit results and customer tier analysis from CS

arguments.for: Enterprise customers require it for compliance (SOC2, FedRAMP prep). Tiered approach minimizes disruption to prosumer users.
arguments.against: Engineering cost same regardless of tier. Universal timeout simplifies security posture. Tier complexity delays ship date.

decisions.title: Invest in Python Rules observability before broader rollout
decisions.context: Files in CEL progressing (ENG-9590), CEL docs being written (ENG-9990), but no visibility into rule performance/errors in production. Critical for 'Embed Deeper' pillar success.

arguments.for: Customers can't debug rules without error traces, limits adoption. Observability is table-stakes for production rules engine. Prevents support escalations.
arguments.against: Slows Python Rules rollout by 3-4 weeks. Current customers managing with eng support. Feature velocity matters more early-stage.

decisions.title: Deprecate legacy alignment point model in Q3 2026
decisions.context: Multiple alignment points per family member now supported (ENG-7789), but maintaining both models creates tech debt. Need migration plan and customer communication strategy.

arguments.for: Cuts maintenance burden 30%, enables faster iteration on time-series features. 6-month notice sufficient for 8 affected customers.
arguments.against: Legacy model serves 40% of enterprise revenue. Migration requires customer eng work during their constrained cycles. Risk churn.

keyResults.name: E2 GA with 90% feature parity to E1
Python Rules adopted by 60% of customers
Files in CEL in production for 5 customers
Data lake storage cost per TB <$15

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy engineering coordination week driven by CSRS parallel workstreams and E2 GA prep. 3 decision-forcing meetings scheduled.

CSRS prioritization discussion has 12 attendees (too many)
